title: OpenAI: GPT-Rosalind Life Sciences Model Introduced To Accelerate Drug Discovery And Research Workflows
summary: OpenAI: GPT-Rosalind Life Sciences Model Introduced To Accelerate Drug Discovery And Research Workflows OpenAI: GPT-Rosalind Life Sciences Model Introduced To Accelerate Drug Discovery And Research Workflows By Amit Chowdhry ● Today at 8:27 AM OpenAI announced the launch of GPT-Rosalind, a purp
content: OpenAI: GPT-Rosalind Life Sciences Model Introduced To Accelerate Drug Discovery And Research Workflows OpenAI: GPT-Rosalind Life Sciences Model Introduced To Accelerate Drug Discovery And Research Workflows By Amit Chowdhry ● Today at 8:27 AM OpenAI announced the launch of GPT-Rosalind, a purpose-built reasoning model designed to support scientific research across biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine. The new model is optimized for complex life sciences workflows, combining advanced reasoning capabilities with improved integration across scientific tools, databases, and ... The introduction of GPT-Rosalind reflects the growing need to address inefficiencies in life sciences research, where the process of developing a new drug can take 10 to 15 years from initial discovery to regulatory approval. Early-stage ... success rates, but these workflows remain fragmented and time-intensive. GPT-Rosalind is designed to help researchers navigate these challenges by enabling faster evidence synthesis, ... otherwise be overlooked. The model is now available as a research preview through ChatGPT, Codex, and the API for qualified users under a trusted access program. OpenAI is also introducing a Life Sciences research plugin for Codex, which connects ... OpenAI is collaborating with leading organizations including Amgen, Moderna, Thermo Fisher Scientific, NVIDIA, the ... To ensure responsible deployment, GPT-Rosalind is being rolled out under a controlled access framework. Organizations ... mitigate risks associated with biological misuse. Looking ahead, OpenAI plans to expand the GPT-Rosalind model series, improving its ability to handle long-horizon and tool-intensive scientific workflows. The company is also working with research institutions, including Los Alamos National Laboratory, to explore applications such as AI-guided protein and catalyst design. content: Anthropic Unveils Claude Design: AI-Powered Tool Transforms Ideas into Visuals Tuesday, 21 April 2026 ... العربية Anthropic Unveils Claude Design: AI-Powered Tool Transforms Ideas into Visuals The experimental platform empowers entrepreneurs and product Abd Al-Fattah Yousef 6 hours ago 183 listen ... Anthropic, a leading artificial intelligence company, has announced the launch of Claude Design, an innovative experimental tool aimed at empowering users to swiftly transform their ideas into professional visual materials. This new AI-powered solution seeks to bridge the gap between initial concepts and visual representation, making it easier ... develop and modify multiple design systems as needed, enhancing efficiency and professionalism. Claude Design is powered by Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 AI model, one of the company's latest and most powerful iterations. The product is currently available as part of a research preview for subscribers of Claude Pro, Claude Max, Claude Team, and Claude Enterprise plans. This rollout strategy indicates Anthropic's focus on professional and institutional segments that increasingly rely on AI tools in their daily operations, reflecting the company's vision to ... title: Amazon to invest an additional $5 billion in Anthropic
summary: Amazon to invest an additional $5 billion in Anthropic - The Japan Times Subscribe Subscribe for more access Amazon to invest an additional $5 billion in Anthropic Anthropic was founded in 2021 by several former employees of OpenAI.
content: Amazon to invest an additional $5 billion in Anthropic - The Japan Times Subscribe Subscribe for more access Amazon to invest an additional $5 billion in Anthropic Anthropic was founded in 2021 by several former employees of OpenAI. | REUTERS Amazon is investing an additional $5 billion in Anthropic, and may inject $20 billion more over time, a deal that deepens the companies’ ties in an increasingly competitive artificial intelligence industry. Anthropic, which makes the Claude chatbot and coding tool, plans to spend more than $100 billion over the next 10 years on Amazon’s cloud technologies and chips, the companies said in a statement on Monday. Amazon shares gained about on the news in extended trading. Amazon was already one of Anthropic’s biggest backers, with prior investments totaling $8 billion. For Amazon, the partnership has given its cloud business a leading artificial intelligence model and a major customer for its home-built Trainium line of AI chips. Anthropic, meanwhile, has had access to Amazon’s massive roster of corporate clients. More than 100,000 customers run Claude models on Amazon Web Services, the companies said. title: xAI Grok Voice APIs Launch April 2026: Did They Just Disrupt the Entire Voice AI Industry?
summary: xAI Grok Voice APIs Launch April 2026: Did They Just Disrupt the Entire Voice AI Industry? - VFuture Media "> "> Published: April 20, 2026 By Ethan Brooks – USA-based Tech Analyst & Futurist On April 17, 2026, xAI quietly dropped what many in the industry are calling a seismic shift in voice AI.
content: xAI Grok Voice APIs Launch April 2026: Did They Just Disrupt the Entire Voice AI Industry? - VFuture Media "> "> Published: April 20, 2026 By Ethan Brooks – USA-based Tech Analyst & Futurist On April 17, 2026, xAI quietly dropped what many in the industry are calling a seismic shift in voice AI. The company released two standalone APIs — Grok Speech-to-Text (STT) and Grok Text-to-Speech (TTS) — built on the same ... Here’s a complete, balanced breakdown of what xAI announced, how it compares to the competition, and what it means for ... What xAI Launched: Grok STT and TTS APIs xAI positioned the new APIs as production-ready tools for building voice agents, transcription services, customer ... - Real-time streaming (WebSocket) and batch processing (REST). - Support for 25+ languages with seamless mid-conversation language switching. ... Both APIs leverage the same underlying infrastructure already running at scale in Tesla cars (for in-vehicle voice ... Aggressive Pricing That Changes the Game xAI’s pricing is the most disruptive element: Speech-to-Text - Batch: $0.10 per hour - Streaming: $0.20 per hour Text-to-Speech - $4.20 per million characters For comparison, competitors charge significantly more: - ElevenLabs, Deepgram, and AssemblyAI typically range from $0.21–$0.55 per hour for STT (batch/streaming). ... xAI claims its offerings are up to 10x cheaper than leading premium solutions and roughly lower than many current ... Benchmark Claims: Outperforming Established Players? xAI reports strong early results: - Phone call entity recognition (names, dates, account numbers): 5.error rate vs. ElevenLabs (12.), Deepgram (13.), and AssemblyAI (21.). ... - Overall word error rate on challenging benchmarks: Around 6.. ... ElevenLabs built a successful business around high-quality, emotionally expressive TTS. Deepgram and AssemblyAI focused on accurate, fast transcription for enterprises. xAI entered the space not by building from scratch for voice alone, ... - Lower barriers for building voice agents, automated customer service, accessibility tools, and content creation ... - Data handling and privacy policies will be scrutinized, especially given xAI’s connection to broader Musk ecosystem ... The Bigger Picture for Voice AI in 2026 xAI’s move reflects a broader trend: companies with massive real-world data and infrastructure advantages (Tesla’s ... xAI Grok Voice APIs Launch April 2026: Did They Just Disrupt the Entire Voice AI Industry? ... title: OpenAI upgrades Codex to control desktops more deeply
summary: OpenAI upgrades Codex to control desktops more deeply - Africa Business Insight SHARE OpenAI has rolled out a major upgrade to its AI coding system Codex, significantly expanding its capabilities and positioning it in d
content: OpenAI upgrades Codex to control desktops more deeply - Africa Business Insight SHARE OpenAI has rolled out a major upgrade to its AI coding system Codex, significantly expanding its capabilities and positioning it in direct competition with Anthropic’s Claude powered coding tools. The update introduces enhanced “agentic” features that allow the system to do more than just generate code, now giving it broader control over user workflows and desktop environments.According to reports, the new version of Codex includes a feature described as a “scratchpad,” which allows the AI to plan, refine, and execute multi step coding tasks more effectively. This upgrade is part of a broader ... digital tasks.The improved Codex is designed to handle more autonomous actions, including interacting with development ... The development highlights the growing competition in the AI industry between major players like OpenAI and Anthropic, both of which are racing to build more capable AI agents for developers. Anthropic’s Claude models have gained traction in coding and reasoning tasks, particularly in enterprise environments, prompting OpenAI to accelerate improvements to Codex.Industry observers say the latest update reflects a broader trend toward “agentic computing,” where AI tools move ... developers write, test, and deploy code, potentially reducing the need for manual oversight in routine tasks.However, the increased power of these systems also raises questions about control, safety, and reliability. Allowing AI tools to ... vulnerabilities, or misuse if safeguards are not properly implemented.OpenAI has not publicly detailed all the guardrails included in the update, but the company has previously emphasized that agentic systems are designed with ... The update also signals a strategic shift in how AI companies are positioning their products. Rather than competing solely on model intelligence, firms are now focusing on integration depth, workflow automation, and real world usability inside professional environments.For developers, the enhanced Codex could mean faster coding cycles, improved automation of repetitive tasks, and more seamless integration between AI assistance and software development tools. For the industry, it represents another step toward AI systems that function less like tools and more like collaborators.As competition between OpenAI and Anthropic intensifies, the race is increasingly about who can build the most powerful ...
